text.title: Behavior Parity Verification Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the shape of the parity report and the diff store. A migration that has not produced these artifacts has not done parity verification — regardless of how much shadow traffic flowed.

list.item: parity-report.md per ramp stage: scope, observable list, ramp window, total compared, diff count, top-5 clusters, sign-off.
parity_diffs table or equivalent store: one row per non-trivial diff with input fingerprint, legacy output hash, new output hash, normalized diff JSON, sampled-at timestamp.
normalizer.{py,ts,go} committed and tested — the function MUST exist as code, not only as a doc.
scope: endpoint or function path under test.
observable_fields: array, copied verbatim from r1 list.
total_compared: integer; gate is >= 1000 per stage typical.
clusters: list of cluster_id, sample_count, root_cause_or_accepted.
signed_off_by + ISO date — required to close stage.

antipattern.description: Diff store keeps raw request/response bodies including PII (emails, names, tokens).
antipattern.reason: Parity infra becomes a PII honeypot. Normalize PII fields to hashes or redact before persisting; observable comparison uses the hash.

antipattern.description: Skipping the diff store and relying on a single "diff_rate" gauge.
antipattern.reason: A gauge tells you something diverged; without per-sample storage you cannot find the cluster or the input that triggered it.

antipattern.description: Auto-promoting between ramp stages by timer alone.
antipattern.reason: r3 requires diff-rate + cluster-review gating. A timer-only promoter is r3 violation and will be tripped by long-tail traffic.

list.item: Observable list written before any shadow traffic.
Diff store schema deployed; PII redaction confirmed.
Each ramp stage has its own report with sign-off.
Cluster analysis present for every non-zero diff stage.
